Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze the Request Input A text based pseudo BPMN representation of a business process Customer Request Handling Task Provide a detailed explanation of the underlying business process Specific Requirements Explain the purpose of each step Explain the meaning of the gateways Explain path convergence and looping Clarify differences between Standard vs Custom requests Clarify conditions for approvals and rework

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Deconstruct the Pseudo BPMN Start Receive Customer Request Decision 1 XOR Check Request Type Standard vs Custom Path A Standard Validation Parallel Checks Credit Inventory Calculate Delivery Date Path B Custom Feasibility Analysis Feasibility Check Yes No If No Rejection End If Yes Prepare Quotation Convergence Point After Standard Path Task D or Custom Path Task E1 Decision 2 XOR Is Approval Needed Path C Yes Obtain Approval Approval Granted Yes No If No Re evaluate Loop back to E1 Custom or D Standard Path D No Approval Needed Generate Final Invoice Convergence After Approval Path Task G or No Approval Path Task G Final Step Send Confirmation End

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Drafting the Explanation Section by Section Introduction Briefly state what the process is Order Fulfillment Order Processing Phase 1 Intake and Routing Start to Gateway 1 Task A Intake Gateway 1 XOR The fork in the road Standard vs Custom logic Phase 2 Divergent Processing Standard vs Custom Standard Validation plus Parallel Checks Credit Inventory plus Delivery Calc Explain why parallel efficiency Custom Feasibility Analysis This is the critical filter If not feasible stop Rejection If feasible Quote Phase 3 Convergence and Approval Logic Both paths meet before the Approval Gateway Gateway 2 XOR Approval Threshold Approval Path Manager sign off Rejection Path Internal If manager says no rework loop Crucial detail Where does it loop back to The diagram says E1 Custom or D Standard I need to explain this nuance re evaluation of conditions No Approval Path Skip to Invoice Phase 4 Finalization Task G Invoice Generation common for both paths Task I Customer Notification End

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Addressing Specific Questions in the Prompt Purpose of each step Map tasks to business value for example Credit Check Risk Management Meaning of gateways Explain XOR Exclusive vs AND Parallel Join Split Convergence Looping Explain how the paths merge and the specific loop condition Approval Denied Standard vs Custom Contrast validation vs feasibility analysis Approval Rework Define the trigger Manager Approval Needed Yes and the consequence Loop if No

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Refining and Polishing Tone Professional analytical clear Formatting Use bolding lists and clear headings to make it readable Correction Verification Wait check the loop The diagram says Loop back to Task E1 Custom or Task D Standard This implies if approval is denied the conditions might change or the quote needs adjustment I need to make sure this is clear Check Convergence The diagram shows After Standard or Custom Path Tasks Completed leading to the Approval Gateway This means Task D and Task E1 are the convergence points before the Approval Gateway Check Task G It appears in two places Approval Yes and No Approval This is a merge point Visual Aid Maybe suggest a visual summary or just describe the flow clearly Text description is sufficient based on prompt

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Review against constraints Did I explain the XOR vs AND Yes Did I explain Standard vs Custom Yes Did I explain the loop Yes Is it detailed Yes

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Constructing the Output Self Correction during drafting Initial thought Just list steps Correction No group them logically Intake Processing Approval Fulfillment Emphasis Highlight the risk management aspect Credit Check and the feasibility aspect Custom Loop Logic Be very specific about what happens if approval is denied It is not just stop it is re evaluate

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Final Polish Check for clarity and flow The resulting output matches the provided good response
